author: Harrison B.
msg:
I completely agree. We need an official response from the Unity team, not just links pointing back to “wait for an update.”

What’s especially frustrating is the lack of transparency. If they would simply say, “Hey everyone, we’re currently focusing all our efforts on Search & Rescue and telemetry, and nothing else is moving forward until those are fully perfected,” most of us would understand and be patient.

Instead, we keep hearing that tasks are ready, customers are lined up, and it’s “days not weeks”, only for nothing to happen. That kind of mixed messaging is draining and makes it feel like something is off behind the scenes.

They could easily just be upfront and say they’re focusing on one rollout at a time. The lack of clear, honest communication is what’s making this so irritating for those of us who paid for UNO access.

At least give a timeline of what’s next. 

If I tell a customer to wait for the next update and expect them to actually sit there patiently for weeks without a resolution.. it’s unreasonable. 

A simple acknowledgment of issues as soon as they arrive for the team to say “ 
we are aware of active errors and are are working closely with our team to provide the next steps for our UNO’s”

author: Alem
msg:
I follow the chats regularly, and I’ll be honest — it can feel quite depressing at times.
Coming from a technical background myself, I’m very familiar with deadlines and high expectations. In my experience, the best antidote to missed targets is **transparency**. Unfortunately, with external dependencies and tight timelines, delivering that transparency gets increasingly difficult.
At this point, we really have two paths forward:
We can either keep trusting the team to deliver (with roughly the same level of trust we had when we bought our nodes), **or** we can work together to build a shared understanding of what “transparency” actually means for everyone here.

One thing I’ve observed is that we should set **different expectations** for different types of work. For example, it’s reasonable to expect relatively accurate delivery dates on things like UI improvements, because the team has full control over them (assuming they’re not relying on a third party).
On the other hand, tasks with many moving parts — like the CLI work — are much harder to pin down precisely. I guess that the Unity development piece was completed before the partners and external dependencies were fully cleared. So the team can release their part, but other pieces are still pending.

Since we have a strong group of developers in this chat, I think it would be valuable to create a simple **development tracker dashboard** (similar to the Cardano Leios one). If the team is open to it, they could give us periodic percentage-based updates on each major task. That alone would help align expectations and cut down on repetitive “Wen, when?” questions. Once a task is at 40 %, there’s no need to ask for daily updates.

Let’s see if there’s enough support here to try it out.

If not… well, Wen will just have to keep changing his username to keep us updated :)

author: Eddie H
msg:
Glad we got an ama, In some ways I would prefer it to be more private so we can have a more open discussion. 

I wonder if there is a way in which we can submit questions and upvote them. So the more pressing questions that we want answers to are focused on and a more detailed explanation is given.

author: Page Øne
msg: lets see 24th looking forward to it

author: Page Øne
msg: #amaquestions just tag it and it will be included

author: Eddie H
msg:
Sorry maybe a bit of miscommunication here. I know you need to hash tag a question but I think it will be helpful to be able to see all the questions submitted and the ability to upvote them. 
For instance If I ask a question about calibration and others think it is important. This question rises towards the top. If I submit a question about If Josh is single and the community do not feel this is relevant. It will not be upvoted and therefore would not take priority/ give a sense of urgency to.

author: Page Øne
msg: ahh okay !

author: Eddie H
msg: I see some people in the community are creating cool dashboards to monitor their rewards and obviously have a good skill set. Maybe we should leverage this? It will make the amas more efficient.

author: Eddie H
msg:
On another note 

I am still running some tests on how to try and improve up time on Apple phones. About a week ago I had a colleague who was struggling to get good uptime. The licence came up with an error. In the end revoked his licence and he reinstalled the unity app. He expressed that he could not change the setting for app refresh. Oddly, his uptime improves significantly and his rewards. 

I tried changing the app reset to be triggered off on another iPhone. Also having problems but did not improve the uptime. 

I tried repeating the process on another phone with a similar issue. I did not have the same result. 

Trying to work out what caused his phone to start running Unity more efficiently.

I used AI to come up with some more suggestions and it suggestedDisable "Offload Unused Apps". I am now trying to explore whether this makes a difference or not. 

I will try and report back if this makes a difference to try and help other people.
